quizData.hook: Only 1% get Question 10 right on this Science Quiz!
quizData.title: Only 1% Can Answer Question 10! 🧠
quizData.description: Challenge your brain with this fast science trivia quiz. Only 1% can score 10/10!

questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria convert nutrients into energy (ATP) for cellular activity.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: H2O represents two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.

questions.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest and closest planet, orbiting in just 88 days.

questions.question: What is the atomic number of Carbon?
questions.explanation: Carbon has 6 protons and is the foundational element for organic chemistry.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in vacuum?
questions.explanation: At this speed, light takes just 1.3 seconds to travel to the Moon.

questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up 78% of our atmosphere, compared to 21% oxygen.

questions.question: What is the rarest naturally occurring element?
questions.explanation: Less than 1 gram of Astatine exists in Earth's crust at any time.

questions.question: What is the half-life of Carbon-14?
questions.explanation: Its half-life makes it perfect for dating organic matter up to 50,000 years old.

questions.question: Which particle has no electric charge?
questions.explanation: Neutrons sit in the nucleus alongside protons and have neutral charge.

questions.question: Who proposed the general theory of relativity?
questions.explanation: General relativity proved gravity is the warping of space and time.
